001package guiEmailRegister;
002
003import javafx.geometry.Pos;
004import javafx.scene.Scene;
005import javafx.scene.control.Alert;
006import javafx.scene.control.Button;
007import javafx.scene.control.Label;
008import javafx.scene.control.PasswordField;
009import javafx.scene.control.TextField;
010import javafx.scene.control.Alert.AlertType;
011import javafx.scene.layout.Pane;
012import javafx.scene.text.Font;
013import javafx.stage.Stage;
014
015/*******
016 * <p> Title: ViewEmailRegister Class. </p>
017 * 
018 * <p> Description: The ViewEmailRegister Page allows users to sign in with their 
019 * email address and create an account with email and password.</p>
020 * 
021 * <p> Copyright: Modified 2026 </p>
022 * 
023 * @version 1.00                2026-02-08 Initial version
024 *  
025 */
026
027public class ViewEmailRegister {
028        
029        /*-********************************************************************************************
030
031        Attributes
032        
033        */
034        
035        // These are the application values required by the user interface
036        
037        private static double width = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_WIDTH;
038        private static double height = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_HEIGHT;
039        
040        private static Label label_ApplicationTitle = 
041                        new Label("Foundation Application Email Registration");
042    protected static Label label_EmailRegistration = new Label("Email Account Registration");
043    protected static Label label_Instructions = new Label("Please enter your email address and create a password.");
044    
045    protected static Label label_Email = new Label("Email Address:");
046    protected static TextField text_Email = new TextField();
047    
048    protected static Label label_Password = new Label("Password:");
049    protected static PasswordField text_Password1 = new PasswordField();
050    
051    protected static Label label_ConfirmPassword = new Label("Confirm Password:");
052    protected static PasswordField text_Password2 = new PasswordField();
053    
054    protected static Label label_Username = new Label("Username:");
055    protected static TextField text_Username = new TextField();
056    
057    protected static Button button_CreateAccount = new Button("Create Account");
058
059        // Alerts for various error conditions
060    protected static Alert alertEmailError = new Alert(AlertType.ERROR);
061        protected static Alert alertPasswordError = new Alert(AlertType.ERROR);
062        protected static Alert alertAccountCreated = new Alert(AlertType.INFORMATION);
063
064    protected static Button button_Cancel = new Button("Cancel");
065
066        // These attributes are used to configure the page and populate it with this user's information
067        private static ViewEmailRegister theView;               // Is instantiation of the class needed?
068
069        protected static Stage theStage;                        // The Stage that JavaFX has established for us
070        private static Pane theRootPane;                        // The Pane that holds all the GUI widgets 
071        public static Scene theEmailRegisterScene = null;       
072        
073        // Invitation context
074        protected static String invitationCode = "";
075        protected static String invitationRole = "";
076        
077
078        /*-********************************************************************************************
079
080        Constructors
081        
082        */
083
084        /**********
085         * <p> Method: displayEmailRegister(Stage ps) </p>
086         * 
087         * <p> Description: This method is the single entry point from outside this package to cause
088         * the EmailRegister page to be displayed.</p>
089         * 
090         * @param ps specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
091         * 
092         */
093        public static void displayEmailRegister(Stage ps) {
094                
095                // Establish the references to the GUI
096                theStage = ps;
097                
098                if (theView == null) theView = new ViewEmailRegister();
099                
100                text_Email.setText("");                 // Clear the input fields
101                text_Email.setEditable(true);
102                text_Username.setText("");
103                text_Password1.setText("");
104                text_Password2.setText("");
105                invitationCode = "";
106                invitationRole = "";
107                
108        // Place all of the established GUI elements into the pane
109        theRootPane.getChildren().clear();
110        theRootPane.getChildren().addAll(
111                        label_ApplicationTitle,
112                        label_EmailRegistration, 
113                        label_Instructions, 
114                        label_Email, text_Email,
115                        label_Username, text_Username,
116                        label_Password, text_Password1,
117                        label_ConfirmPassword, text_Password2, 
118                        button_CreateAccount, 
119                        button_Cancel);         
120
121                // Set the title for the window, display the page
122                theStage.setTitle("CSE 360 Foundation Code: Email Registration");       
123        theStage.setScene(theEmailRegisterScene);
124                theStage.show();
125        }
126
127        /**********
128         * <p> Method: displayEmailRegister(Stage ps, String email, String code, String role) </p>
129         * 
130         * <p> Description: This method displays the EmailRegister page with the email prefilled from
131         * an invitation code and locks the email field.</p>
132         * 
133         * @param ps specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
134         * @param email the invited email address to prefill
135         * @param code the invitation code
136         * @param role the invited role
137         */
138        public static void displayEmailRegister(Stage ps, String email, String code, String role) {
139                theStage = ps;
140                if (theView == null) theView = new ViewEmailRegister();
141                text_Email.setText(email == null ? "" : email);
142                text_Email.setEditable(false);
143                text_Username.setText("");
144                text_Password1.setText("");
145                text_Password2.setText("");
146                invitationCode = code == null ? "" : code;
147                invitationRole = role == null ? "" : role;
148
149                theRootPane.getChildren().clear();
150                theRootPane.getChildren().addAll(
151                                label_ApplicationTitle,
152                                label_EmailRegistration, 
153                                label_Instructions, 
154                                label_Email, text_Email,
155                                label_Username, text_Username,
156                                label_Password, text_Password1,
157                                label_ConfirmPassword, text_Password2, 
158                                button_CreateAccount, 
159                                button_Cancel);
160
161                theStage.setTitle("CSE 360 Foundation Code: Email Registration");
162                theStage.setScene(theEmailRegisterScene);
163                theStage.show();
164        }
165        
166        /**********
167         * <p> Constructor: ViewEmailRegister() </p>
168         * 
169         * <p> Description: This constructor is called just once, the first time email registration 
170         * needs to be performed.</p>
171         * 
172         */
173        private ViewEmailRegister() {
174
175                // Create the Pane for the list of widgets and the Scene for the window
176                theRootPane = new Pane();
177                theEmailRegisterScene = new Scene(theRootPane, width, height);
178                
179                // Populate the window with the title and other widgets
180                setupLabelUI(label_ApplicationTitle, "Arial", 28, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 10);
181
182                setupLabelUI(label_EmailRegistration, "Arial", 24, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 60);
183                
184                setupLabelUI(label_Instructions, "Arial", 16, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 100);
185
186                // Email field
187                setupLabelUI(label_Email, "Arial", 18, 150, Pos.BASELINE_RIGHT, 200, 150);
188                setupTextUI(text_Email, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 360, 145, true);
189                text_Email.setPromptText("Enter your email");
190
191                // Username field
192                setupLabelUI(label_Username, "Arial", 18, 150, Pos.BASELINE_RIGHT, 200, 200);
193                setupTextUI(text_Username, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 360, 195, true);
194                text_Username.setPromptText("Choose a username");
195
196                // Password field
197                setupLabelUI(label_Password, "Arial", 18, 150, Pos.BASELINE_RIGHT, 200, 250);
198                setupTextUI(text_Password1, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 360, 245, true);
199                text_Password1.setPromptText("Enter password");
200
201                // Confirm password field
202                setupLabelUI(label_ConfirmPassword, "Arial", 18, 150, Pos.BASELINE_RIGHT, 200, 300);
203                setupTextUI(text_Password2, "Arial", 18, 300, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 360, 295, true);
204                text_Password2.setPromptText("Confirm password");
205
206                // Set up the Create Account button
207                setupButtonUI(button_CreateAccount, "Dialog", 18, 200, Pos.CENTER, 350, 360);
208                button_CreateAccount.setStyle("-fx-background-color: #4285F4; -fx-text-fill: white;");
209                button_CreateAccount.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerEmailRegister.doCreateAccount(theStage); });
210
211                // Set up alerts
212                alertEmailError.setTitle("Invalid Email");
213                alertEmailError.setHeaderText(null);
214                
215                alertPasswordError.setTitle("Password Error");
216                alertPasswordError.setHeaderText(null);
217                
218                alertAccountCreated.setTitle("Account Created");
219                alertAccountCreated.setHeaderText("Your account has been created successfully!");
220
221                // Set up the Cancel button  
222                setupButtonUI(button_Cancel, "Dialog", 18, 200, Pos.CENTER, 350, 420);
223                button_Cancel.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerEmailRegister.performCancel(theStage); });
224        }
225
226        /*-*******************************************************************************************
227
228        Helper methods used to minimize the number of lines of code needed
229        
230        */
231
232        /**********
233         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
234         */
235        private void setupLabelUI(Label l, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
236                l.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
237                l.setMinWidth(w);
238                l.setAlignment(p);
239                l.setLayoutX(x);
240                l.setLayoutY(y);                
241        }
242        
243        /**********
244         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
245         */
246        private void setupButtonUI(Button b, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
247                b.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
248                b.setMinWidth(w);
249                b.setAlignment(p);
250                b.setLayoutX(x);
251                b.setLayoutY(y);                
252        }
253
254        /**********
255         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a text input field
256         */
257        private void setupTextUI(TextField t, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y, boolean e){
258                t.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
259                t.setMinWidth(w);
260                t.setMaxWidth(w);
261                t.setAlignment(p);
262                t.setLayoutX(x);
263                t.setLayoutY(y);                
264                t.setEditable(e);
265        }       
266}
